disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient has a known autoimmune condition (lupus) and reports the current symptoms flared the day after brief sun exposure.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any ongoing health conditions?
Did anything seem to set this off?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got lupus, only diagnosed recently. This all flared up the day after I was out in the sun for a bit.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetrical pain and swelling across both wrists, the knuckles, the small joints of the hands and both knees.
trigger_question_examples: Is it one joint or several, and is it the same on both sides?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's both wrists, my knuckles, the little joints in my hands and both knees — the same on both sides, all swollen and sore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A red, slightly raised, burning rash across both cheeks and the bridge of the nose, made worse by light.
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the rash and does light affect it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's a bright red rash over both my cheeks and the bridge of my nose, and it burns. Light makes it worse, so I've kept the room dark.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound fatigue with functional collapse — bedbound, unable to reach the kitchen or prepare food.
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting what you can do?
Are you able to look after yourself at the moment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm completely wiped out and stuck in bed. I can't get to the kitchen or make food because my hands and joints hurt too much.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels feverish; a low-grade temperature (37.4 C) rather than a high spiking fever with rigors.
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers or chills?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel a bit feverish and it read 37.4 on my thermometer, but I haven't had shaking chills or a really high temperature.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No single joint is disproportionately worse, hotter or redder than the others; the pattern is even and symmetrical.
trigger_question_examples: Is any one joint much worse, hotter or redder than the rest?
Is one joint far more swollen than the others?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's pretty even across all of them — there isn't one joint that's much worse or hotter than the rest.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sharp chest pain on breathing in and no shortness of breath.
trigger_question_examples: Any sharp pain in your chest when you breathe in?
Are you short of breath at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my breathing is fine and I don't get any sharp pain in my chest when I breathe.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Currently taking hydroxychloroquine regularly; took paracetamol today with no relief.
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken anything for the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I take hydroxychloroquine every day for the lupus, and I tried paracetamol today but it did absolutely nothing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone and cannot currently prepare food or manage basic self-care because of the pain.
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone at home with you?
Are you managing to eat and drink and look after yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own. Tonight I can't really get to the kitchen or make anything to eat because my hands are too painful.
